article.description: Baltimore Orioles (35-42, fourth in the AL East) vs. Los Angeles Dodgers (49-27, first in the NL West)
article.headline: Dodgers look to keep home win streak going, host the Orioles
article.linkText: Dodgers look to keep home win streak going, host the Orioles
article.story: Baltimore Orioles (35-42, fourth in the AL East) vs. Los Angeles Dodgers (49-27, first in the NL West) Los Angeles; Saturday, 10:10 p.m. EDT PITCHING PROBABLES: Orioles: Trevor Rogers (3-7, 5.86 ERA, 1.46 WHIP, 48 strikeouts); Dodgers: Yoshinobu Yamamoto (7-4, 2.52 ERA, 0.84 WHIP, 80 strikeouts) LINE: Dodgers -274, Orioles +218; over/under is 8 1/2 runs BOTTOM LINE: The Los Angeles Dodgers, on a four-game home winning streak, host the Baltimore Orioles. Los Angeles is 49-27 overall and 26-12 at home. The Dodgers have the second-best team ERA in MLB play at 3.35. Baltimore is 13-23 in road games and 35-42 overall. The Orioles have a 29-8 record in games when they scored at least five runs. The matchup Saturday is the second time these teams match up this season. TOP PERFORMERS: Shohei Ohtani has 15 home runs, 51 walks and 42 RBI while hitting .296 for the Dodgers. Mookie Betts is 13 for 43 with three doubles, two home runs and two RBI over the last 10 games. Pete Alonso leads the Orioles with 17 home runs while slugging .467. Gunnar Henderson is 8 for 37 with three home runs and six RBI over the past 10 games. LAST 10 GAMES: Dodgers: 7-3, .260 batting average, 4.55 ERA, outscored opponents by 12 runs Orioles: 4-6, .237 batting average, 4.18 ERA, outscored by three runs INJURIES: Dodgers: Bobby Miller: 60-Day IL (shoulder), Brock Stewart: 15-Day IL (foot), Evan Phillips: 60-Day IL (elbow), Will Smith: 10-Day IL (neck), Teoscar Hernandez: 10-Day IL (hamstring), Gavin Stone: 60-Day IL (shoulder), Ben Casparius: 60-Day IL (shoulder), Tyler Glasnow: 60-Day IL (back), Brusdar Graterol: 60-Day IL (shoulder), Blake Snell: 60-Day IL (elbow), Kike Hernandez: 10-Day IL (oblique), Landon Knack: 60-Day IL (chest), Edwin Diaz: 60-Day IL (elbow), Jake Cousins: 60-Day IL (elbow) Orioles: Adley Rutschman: day-to-day (head), Dean Kremer: 60-Day IL (quadricep), Cade Povich: 15-Day IL (elbow), Ryan Mountcastle: 60-Day IL (foot), Chris Bassitt: 15-Day IL (back), Dylan Beavers: 10-Day IL (oblique), Yaramil Hiraldo: 60-Day IL (shoulder), Zach Eflin: 60-Day IL (elbow), Felix Bautista: 60-Day IL (shoulder), Colin Selby: 60-Day IL (shoulder), Jordan Westburg: 60-Day IL (ucl) ------ The Associated Press created this story using technology provided by Data Skrive and data from Sportradar.

articles.description: The Diamondbacks lost another starting pitcher to injury on Saturday, placing right-hander Michael Soroka on the 15-day injured list with a strained right glute.
articles.headline: Soroka latest Diamondbacks starter to hit injured list

articles.description: Washington Nationals vs. Tampa Bay Rays: Game Highlights
articles.headline: Washington Nationals vs. Tampa Bay Rays: Game Highlights

images.caption: Washington Nationals vs. Tampa Bay Rays: Game Highlights
images.name: Washington Nationals vs. Tampa Bay Rays: Game Highlights

articles.description: The Red Sox placed utility infielder Isiah Kiner-Falefa on the injured list Saturday with left forearm inflammation.
articles.headline: Red Sox place Kiner-Falefa on IL with forearm inflammation

articles.description: — Max Meyer allowed two runs in five innings to win his eighth consecutive decision to start the season, Heriberto Hernández homered and the Miami Marlins beat the San Francisco Giants 6-3 on Saturday for their seventh straight victory at home.
articles.headline: Meyer wins 8th straight decision to start season as Marlins beat Giants 6-3

images.caption: San Francisco Giants vs. Miami Marlins: Game Highlights
images.name: San Francisco Giants vs. Miami Marlins: Game Highlights

articles.description: — Manny Machado hit a three-run homer, his 13th this season, in the 10th inning and tied his season high of five RBI, helping the San Diego Padres rally for a 6-4 victory over the Texas Rangers on Saturday.
articles.headline: Manny Machado hits a 3-run homer in 10th and has 5 RBIs as Padres beat Rangers 6-4

images.caption: Wyatt Langford hits an RBI double
images.name: Wyatt Langford hits an RBI double
